Uses of Interface
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.CollectionVerifier

Packages that use CollectionVerifier
Package
Description
com.github.cowwoc.requirements
The Requirements, DefaultRequirements endpoints.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.guava
Classes used to verify preconditions, postconditions and invariants of Guava types.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Classes used to verify preconditions, postconditions and invariants of core Java types.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal
Design guide

    Method
    Description

    Verifies the requirements of a Collection.

    Method
    Description

    Returns a verifier over Multimap.entries().

    Returns a verifier over Multimap.keySet().

  • Uses of CollectionVerifier in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java that return CollectionVerifier
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.asArray(Class<E> type, Consumer<ArrayVerifier<E[],E>> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,E>
    ArrayVerifier.asCollection()
    Returns a verifier for the actual value as a collection.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.contains(E element)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.contains(E expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.containsAll(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.containsAll(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.containsAny(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.containsAny(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.containsExactly(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.containsExactly(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContain(E element)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContain(E element, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainDuplicates()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.doesNotContainMixedNulls()
    Ensures that the actual value contains only null values, or only non-null values.
    CollectionVerifier<Set<Map.Entry<K,V>>,Map.Entry<K,V>>
    MapVerifier.entrySet()
    Returns a verifier for the Map.entrySet().
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    CollectionVerifier<Set<K>,K>
    MapVerifier.keySet()
    Returns a verifier for the Map.keySet().
    <C extends Collection<E>, E>
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    DefaultJavaRequirements.requireThat(C actual, String name)
     
    <C extends Collection<E>, E>
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    JavaRequirements.requireThat(C actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a Collection.
    CollectionVerifier<C,E>
    CollectionVerifier.size(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    CollectionVerifier<Collection<V>,V>
    MapVerifier.values()
    Returns a verifier for the Map.values().
